title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. persbergense
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. persbergense, also known as hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is rich in flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits, including antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Hieracium vulgatum subsp. persbergense can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used topically or internally to promote healing and well-being.
